description: This file defines API endpoints for managing assets in an inventory management backend. It includes routes for listing, creating, and updating assets, utilizing FastAPI and dependencies for authentication and database interactions.

methods.name: list[AssetResponse] list_assets( q: str | None = None, limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0, assets_repo: AssetsRepository = Depends(), _: dict = Depends(get_current_user), )
methods.description: GET endpoint to retrieve a list of assets with optional query parameters for filtering and pagination.

methods.name: AssetResponse create_asset( payload: AssetCreateRequest, assets_repo: AssetsRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
methods.description: POST endpoint to create a new asset, restricted to admin users, with conflict handling if asset_tag exists.

methods.name: AssetResponse update_asset( asset_id: str, payload: AssetUpdateRequest, assets_repo: AssetsRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
methods.description: PATCH endpoint to update fields of an existing asset, restricted to admin users, with 404 handling if asset not found.

methods.name: dict _asset_api(doc: dict)
methods.description: Helper function to convert asset document dict into a serializable format, formatting datetime fields to ISO string.

ctags.name: create_asset
ctags.signature: ( payload: AssetCreateRequest, assets_repo: AssetsRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )

ctags.name: list_assets
ctags.signature: ( q: str | None = None, limit: int = 100, offset: int = 0, assets_repo: AssetsRepository = Depends(), _: dict = Depends(get_current_user), )

ctags.name: update_asset
ctags.signature: ( asset_id: str, payload: AssetUpdateRequest, assets_repo: AssetsRepository = Depends(), audits_repo: AuditsRepository = Depends(), actor: dict = Depends(require_roles(RoleName.admin)), )
